q: How many musicians are usually in a string quartet?
exp: Two violins, a viola and a cello.

q: How many lines does a musical staff have?
exp: Five lines and four spaces show the pitch of notes.

q: Which interval separates two notes of the same name (e.g. C to C)?
exp: Across an octave, the note's frequency doubles.

q: Which instrument did Adolphe Sax invent around 1840?
exp: It is made of brass but played with a reed, like a clarinet.

q: Which Italian word marks a very slow tempo?
exp: Presto and vivace, by contrast, mean fast.

q: Which band released 'The Dark Side of the Moon' in 1973?
exp: It is one of the best-selling albums of all time.

q: What is the highest female singing voice called?
exp: Among male voices, the lowest is the bass.

q: Which Russian composer wrote the ballet 'Swan Lake'?
What does the time signature 4/4 indicate?
exp: 4/4 is the most common time signature in popular music.

q: How many sharps does the key of G major have?
exp: G major has F sharp; C major has no sharps or flats.

q: Which composer became deaf yet kept composing masterpieces?
exp: He composed the 9th Symphony while almost completely deaf.

q: Which voice type is the lowest male singing voice?
exp: From high to low: tenor, baritone, bass.
